title: Roku Q3 2024 Earnings Summary
text: Roku achieved a major milestone by posting its first quarter with over $1 billion in net revenue, reaching $1.06 billion and representing 16% year-over-year growth. The company significantly improved its financial position with a net loss of just $9.03 million, down from $330.1 million in the prior year. The streaming platform operates across 85.5 million households globally. Platform revenue, primarily driven by advertising and subscription splits, grew 15% year-over-year to $908.2 million. Device revenue increased 23% to $154 million. Streaming engagement remained robust, with 32 billion total hours viewed, up 20.3% annually. Daily average streaming per household reached 4.1 hours compared to 3.9 hours previously. Beginning in Q1 2025, Roku will discontinue reporting streaming households and average revenue-per-user metrics. According to management, "the majority of our Platform revenue is currently generated in the U.S." while household growth concentrates internationally, making these metrics less representative of actual business performance. The company expects to reach 100 million households within 12-18 months and anticipates Q4 2024 revenue of approximately $1.14 billion with 16% growth. Despite strong results, stock prices fell over 10% following the announcement.
